title: FINRA Case 25-00503 — Daniel Raupp v. Concorde Investment Services, LLC
text:
Expungement Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimants Case Number: 25-00503
Daniel Philip Raupp
vs.
Respondents Hearing Site: New York, New York
Concorde Investment Services, LLC
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are appointed pursuant to Rule 13806 of
the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”) to issue final, binding decisions. FINRA makes
available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by the SEC—but has no part in
deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Associated Person vs. Member
The expungement hearing was conducted by videoconference.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
For Claimant Daniel Philip Raupp: Jennifer Cox, Esq., HLBS Law, Broomfield, Colorado.
For Respondent Concorde Investment Services, LLC: Nicole Woolson, Esq., Concorde
Investment Services, LLC, Ann Arbor, Michigan.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: June 19, 2025.
Daniel Philip Raupp signed the Submission Agreement: June 19, 2025.
Statement of Answer filed by Respondent on or about: August 27, 2025
Concorde Investment Services, LLC signed the Submission Agreement: September 10, 2025.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted a claim seeking expungement of customer dispute
information from registration records maintained by the Central Registration Depository (“CRD”).
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent supported Claimant’s expungement request.
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant (CRD Number 4079107) requested: expungement of all

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-00503
Award Page 2 of 6
references to Occurrence Numbers 2038143, 2089534, 2104389, 2126485, 2148261, 2214563,
2258951, and 2299693 from the CRD registration records pursuant to FINRA Rule
2080(b)(1)(A), 2080(b)(1)(B)(only as to Occurrence Number 2038143), and 2080(b)(1)(C);
deletion of all Disclosure Reporting Pages accompanying the Occurrences; and any and all
other relief that the Panel deems just and equitable.
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent requested that no fees be assessed against the firm.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrators acknowledge that they have each read the pleadings and other materials filed by
the parties.
On July 28, 2025, Claimant advised that the customers in Occurrence Number 2104389
(“Customers A”), 2258951 (“Customers B”), 2299693 (“Customer C”), 2126485 (“Customer D”),
2089534, 2038143, 2148261, 2214563 (collectively “Customers”) were served with a copy of the
Statement of Claim, although not within 10 days of filing the Statement of Claim, and provided
proof of service.
On September 17, 2025, Claimant advised that the Customers were served with a copy of the
Statement of Answer within 10 days of its filing and provided proof of service.
During the evidentiary hearing, Claimant represented that Customers B sent Claimant an email
supporting Claimant’s request for expungement. At the hearing, the Panel requested that Claimant
file a copy of the email for panel review. As of the date of this award, Claimant has not submitted a
copy of the email supporting expungement.
The Panel conducted a recorded hearing by videoconference on February 24, 2025, so the parties
could present oral argument and evidence on Claimant’s request for expungement. Claimant
appeared by videoconference at the expungement hearing. Respondent did not appear at the
expungement hearing.
Customer C appeared at the expungement hearing by videoconference and did not oppose the
expungement request. Customer D appeared at the expungement hearing by videoconference and
supported the expungement request. The remaining Customers did not appear at the
expungement hearing.
No authorized representative of a state securities regulator appeared at the expungement hearing.
The Panel noted Claimant’s representation that a prior arbitration panel or court has not
previously ruled on expungement of the same occurrences in the CRD.
The Panel reviewed the settlement documentation related to Occurrence Numbers 2258951,
2104389, and 2299693, considered the amount of payment made to any party to the
settlements, and considered other relevant terms and conditions of the settlements. The Panel
noted that the settlements were not conditioned on any party to the settlements not opposing
the expungement request and that Claimant did not contribute to the settlement amounts.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-00503
Award Page 3 of 6
The Panel relied upon the following documentary or other evidence: Claimant's Statement of
Claim, Respondent's Statement of Answer, the testimony of Claimant and Claimant’s Expert
Witness, and Claimant's exhibits.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the testimony and evidence presented at the expungement
hearing, and any post-hearing submissions, the Panel has decided in full and final resolution of
the issues submitted for determination as follows:
1. Claimant’s request for expungement of Occurrence Numbers 2126485, 2089534, 2038143,
2148261, and 2214563 from registration records maintained by the CRD is denied with
prejudice. The Panel’s decision was unanimous.
2. The Panel awards the expungement of all references to Occurrence Numbers 2258951,
2104389, and 2299693 from registration records maintained by the CRD for Claimant (CRD
Number 4079107) with the understanding that, pursuant to Rule 2080, Claimant must obtain
confirmation from a court of competent jurisdiction before the CRD will execute the
expungement directive.
Unless specifically waived in writing by FINRA, parties seeking judicial confirmation of an
arbitration award containing customer dispute expungement relief must name FINRA as an
additional party and serve FINRA with all appropriate documents.
Pursuant to Rule 13805 of the Code, the Panel has made the following affirmative finding of
fact:
The claim, allegation, or information is false.
The Panel has made the above finding based on the following reasons:
The Respondent, in its Statement of Answer, stated that "Concorde supports [Claimant’s]
requests for expungement."
The Panel has denied expungement for the following five occurrence numbers:
2038143, 2089534, 2126485, 2148261, and 2214563.
The Panel has granted expungement for the following three occurrence numbers:
Occurrence Number 2104389: Customers A alleged that Claimant placed them in
unsuitable investments with an overconcentration in illiquid investment accounts due to
their age. Claimant testified and the exhibits showed that he did his due diligence to get
to know his customers and recommended suitable investments according to their stated
investment goals. The Panel finds Customers A’s version of events not credible and that
Customers A’s allegations were not supported by the evidence, and are therefore, false.
For these reasons, expungement is appropriate.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-00503
Award Page 4 of 6
Occurrence Number 2258951: Customers B alleged that the Claimant in recommending
their investments made: material misrepresentations, investment recommendations were
not suitable, violated his fiduciary duty, and failed to supervise. The Claimant's testimony
and the Claimant's exhibits showed that Customers B wanted a high-risk investment, and
their alternative investments were under 30% of their investments. Customers B filed a
FINRA Arbitration claim in order to recoup part of their investment, and the Respondent
settled the case as a business decision. Customers B are still customers of the Claimant,
and sent an email supporting expungement for Claimant in their case. The Panel finds
that the Claimant did his due diligence, recommended suitable investments and acted in
Customers B’s best interest. There was no wrongdoing on the part of Claimant, and
therefore the Panel finds Customers B’s allegations false and recommends
expungement.
Occurrence Number 2299693: Customer C alleged that Claimant recommended
unsuitable investments. Customer C took no further action without filing a FINRA
Arbitration claim. Respondent closed the matter with no action taken. Customer C
appeared at the Expungement Hearing and stated that he was not mad at Claimant, he is
still a customer and in favor of expungement of his allegations. Claimant's testimony and
the Claimant's exhibits showed that Claimant did his due diligence on his customer’s
goals and that the recommended investments were suitable. The Panel finds that
Customer C’s allegations to be false and recommends expungement.
3. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 2,000.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm that employed the associated person at the time of the events giving rise to the
dispute. Accordingly, as a party, Respondent is assessed the following:
Member Surcharge =$ 2,600.00
Member Process Fee =$ 5,005.00
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Panel has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrators, including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrators, which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-00503
Award Page 5 of 6
One (1) pre-hearing session with the Panel @ $1,725.00/session =$ 1,725.00
Pre-Hearing Conference: September 22, 2025 1 session
Two (2) hearing sessions on expungement request @ $1,725.00/session =$ 3,450.00
Hearing: February 24, 2026 2 sessions
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 5,175.00
The Panel has assessed the total hearing session fees to Claimant.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-00503
Award Page 6 of 6
ARBITRATION PANEL
Michael J. Ahlstrom - Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson
Douglas J. Nesbit - Public Arbitrator
Martin L. Feinberg - Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm, pursuant to Article 7507 of the Civil Practice Law
and Rules, that I am the individual described herein and who executed this instrument, which is
my award.
Concurring Arbitrators' Signatures
